latest.title: Total Voting Rights
latest.content:
Today 11:00
RNS Number : 4675G
Tullow Oil PLC
01 June 2026
1 June 2026
Tullow Oil plc
Total Voting Rights
In conformity with the FCA's Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rules, Tullow Oil plc today announces the following:
As at 31 May 2026, Tullow Oil plc's issued share capital consisted of 1,515,023,041 ordinary shares (with a nominal value of 10p each) with voting rights. No ordinary shares were held in treasury.
Therefore, the total number of voting rights in Tullow Oil plc was 1,515,023,041.
The above figure may be used by shareholders as the denominator for the calculation by which they will determine if they are required to notify their interest in, or a change to their interest in, Tullow Oil plc under the FCA's Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rules.

latest.title: Block Listing Application
latest.content:
Today 11:22
RNS Number : 3035F
Tullow Oil PLC
21 May 2026
21 May 2026
Tullow Oil plc
("
Tullow Oil
" or the "Company")
Block Listing Application
A block listing application has been made
to the London Stock Exchange for trading on the main market
for a block admission of 18,000,000 ordinary shares of 10p each in the Company
.
The new ordinary shares will be issued from time to time to satisfy the issue of shares pursuant to the exercise of options under the Company's
Employee Share Award Plan (8,000,000) and the Tullow Incentive Plan (10,000,000).
It is expected that admission will become effective on or around 27 May 2026. The shares will be issued fully paid and will rank pari passu in all respects with the existing issued Ordinary Shares of the Company.

latest.title: Publication of Annual Report and Notice of AGM
latest.content:
Today 07:00
RNS Number : 4831C
Tullow Oil PLC
30 April 2026
Tullow Oil plc
Publication of the 2025 Annual Report and Notice of Annual General Meeting
30 April 2026
-
Following the publication on 28 April 2026 of Tullow Oil Plc's ("the Company") Full Year Results for the year ended 31 December 2025, the Company announces it has published its Annual Report and Accounts for this period (the "Annual Report").
The Company's 2026 Annual General Meeting ("AGM") will be held at the Company's registered address at 9 Chiswick Park, 566 Chiswick High Road, London W4 5XT on 10 June 2026 at 11.00am.
In compliance with 9.6.1 and 9.6.3 of the Listing Rules and 6.3.5 of the Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rules, a copy of the Annual Report is being submitted to the National Storage Mechanism, in addition to regulated information in full unedited text will be available for viewing shortly at:
https://data.fca.org.uk/#/nsm/nationalstoragemechanism
. The copy of the Annual Report and the Notice of 2026 AGM are available to view on Tullow's website:
www.tullowoil.com
.
In addition the above documents have been submitted to the Ghana Stock Exchange, and therefore will shortly be available to shareholders located in Ghana by contacting the Company's registrar: Central Securities Depository (GH) Limited, 4th Floor, Cedi House, PMB CT 465 Cantonments, Accra, Ghana (Telephone: +233 (0)302 906 576).
CONTACTS
Tullow Investor Relations
ir@tullowoil.com
Matthew Evans
Camarco (Media)
(+44 20 3757 4980)
Billy Clegg
Georgia Edmonds
Rebecca Waterworth
Notes to editors
Tullow is an independent energy company committed to building a better future through the responsible oil and gas development of its core producing assets in Ghana. The Group is quoted on the London and Ghanaian stock exchanges (symbol: TLW). For further information, please refer to:
www.tullowoil.com
.
Follow Tullow on:
LinkedIn:
www.linkedin.com/company/Tullow-Oil
X:
www.X.com/TullowOilplc
This information is provided by RNS, the news service of the London Stock Exchange. RNS is approved by the Financial Conduct Authority to act as a Primary Information Provider in the United Kingdom. company_30s.what_they_do: Tullow Oil plc is an international oil and gas exploration company that searches for and develops new oil fields, mainly in West Africa and the Middle East.
company_30s.why_they_matter: Oil and gas remain a cornerstone of the global energy supply. Tullow’s projects are small‑to‑mid‑size but often located in high‑potential regions, giving the company a chance to add significant production when commodity prices recover.
company_30s.current_state: The company has been under pressure from low oil prices, which has pushed its share price far below its all‑time highs. Recent filings show it has just finished a refinancing deal, appointed four new independent directors, and filed a full annual report. These moves suggest management is tightening its balance sheet and preparing for a turnaround, but the stock is still trading at a steep discount to its historic valuation.

rns_narrative: Tullow completed a successful refinancing transaction, giving it fresh capital and a stronger debt profile.
The board has been refreshed with four new independent directors, signalling a potential shift in strategy and governance.
An annual report and AGM notice were issued, indicating the company is moving forward with its corporate governance and shareholder communications.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Mixed chatter with a slight tilt toward cautious optimism—investors are watching for any signs the company can turn its operations around.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: Some traders point to the new board and refinancing as a signal that management is ready to take decisive action and that a recovery is possible if oil prices rise.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Others warn that the company still faces high operating costs, uncertain oil price outlook, and the risk that its projects may not become commercial in time.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The tone has shifted from outright pessimism to a more measured watch‑and‑wait stance, but overall enthusiasm remains low.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends for the ticker is flat—no recent spikes in search interest, which is a good sign for a contrarian play because the crowd is largely unaware.

near_term_catalysts: If oil prices climb above $60 a barrel, production from Tullow’s existing fields could become profitable, sparking a price rally.
A positive update on any of its West African projects (e.g., a new discovery or a production licence) would provide a concrete catalyst.
Completion of the refinancing and the first tranche of new debt repayments would improve the balance sheet and reduce interest costs.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a spike in trading volume that exceeds the 3‑month average, a break above the 50‑day moving average, or a clear statement from the CEO about a new project milestone.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock appears to be in the early stages of a capitulation cycle—prices have dropped sharply, volume has thinned, and the market has largely forgotten it. A mean‑reversion play would need a clear signal that the bottom is reached before the price can start climbing.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: Tullow sits well below its historical price because it is in a deep crash zone (roughly 70‑95% down). The recent refinancing and board changes remove the biggest structural risk. If oil prices recover and the company delivers a production or development milestone, the share could rebound to the midpoint of its historical range, delivering roughly 3‑to‑4 times the current price. The color hierarchy points to a high‑reward upside: a RED signal would historically give 160%+ returns, and a PURPLE accumulation would boost that further.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: Oil price volatility remains a core risk; if prices stay low, the company’s projects may never become profitable. Even with a better balance sheet, the company still has a large debt load and a history of cash burn. A worst‑case scenario would see the share price decline to its lowest historical level, potentially erasing most of the current investment.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential upside is roughly 3× current price, while downside is limited to a small percentage of the entry point if a stop is placed just below the recent low. The asymmetry is strong: a modest loss could be offset by a large gain if the recovery hits the midpoint of the historical range.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter on a pullback that closes above the 20‑day moving average, with a position size that allows a stop 5% below the entry to protect against a brief dip. Use a trailing stop at 10% below the peak once the price starts to climb.
playbook.if_watching: Wait for a volume spike that is at least 50% higher than the 3‑month average and a break of the 50‑day moving average. Alternatively, a clear CEO statement about a new production milestone or a favorable oil price movement would be a trigger.
playbook.if_passing: There are no imminent delisting or bankruptcy risks. The main terminal risk is a prolonged oil price slump; if that occurs, the trade would likely stay in the loss zone.

ai_insights.bottom_line: Tullow Oil is a deep‑crashed play that has just strengthened its balance sheet; if oil prices recover and a project milestone is announced, the stock could rebound to the midpoint of its historic range, offering a highly asymmetric upside.
